content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive Legal . . . Enforcement Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5521 322-8441

James M. Gass, President
California Association of Independent Business
201-203 South Fern Avenue
Ontario, California 91761

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-031

Dear Mr. Gass:

Thank you for your letter of March 2, 1976, asking for
an opinion concerning your authorization to act as a lobbyist.
Because your letter raises no substantial question involving
an interpretation of the Political Reform Act, no opinion
will be issued. However, I hope the following informal
comments will be helpful.

Government Code Section 86101 requires every lobbyist
to file "a written authorization to act as a lobbyist from
each person by whom he is employed or with whom he contracts
....". The term "person" is defined by Section 82047 to in-
clude not only natural persons but also an "association,
committee, and any other organization or group of persons
acting in concert." The Commission has adopted a regulation
stating that when a lobbyist is employed by a bona fide
association, the members of the association are not employers
of a lobbyist. 2 Cal.Adm. Code Section 18618. Consequently,
when you lobby on behalf of the California Association of
Independent Business, you must comply with Section 86101 by
filing an authorization from the Association, but you do not
need to file an authorization from every member of the Asso-
ciation.

Please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal
Division of the Commission staff, if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman,
For the Commission

DHL:pvp

California Association of Independent Business

TELEPHONE (714) 995-6422

STATE HEADQUARTERS
405 S. FERN AVENUE
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91761

MAILING
P.O. BOX 1241
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91762

14 1975

Religious Practices Commission
1109 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Request for opinion with regard to reporting on forms #620 -#650

Commissioners:

I need to know the basis upon which I am to report under "Salaries,
Fees, Retainers" to make certain that I am doing this properly.
I am a registered lobbyist for the Association and I am the sole
officer of the Association as well as a small business of just like
those businesses that we represent.

As I see it, there are four ways that I could report in the
column "Salaries, Fees, Retainers":

1. I could report actual earnings - monies taken in by the Association, less expenses ( by personal gross income )

2. I could report a percentage of actual earnings - a percentage
of the monies taken in by the Association, less expense ( a percentage
of my personal gross income )

3. I could report all monies received by the Association during
a particular month.

4. I could report a percentage of all monies taken in by the
Association during a particular month.

I have been told by FPPC that number #2 was correct - then I was
told by FPD that number #3 was correct - then I was told by FPPC that
number #3 might be correct after all - I was told by the Secretary of
State's office that number #2 is correct. Hence, you can see why I
need your formal opinion.

The Association does basically four things: 1. We provide information service to members with regard to bills and issues
or disagreement. This is usually done by a lobbyist but not always.
We include much editorial opinion and offer information and reports
to the author for their general interest. 2. We send the members
witch-of-opinion ballots featuring legislation and issues so that they
can give us, we send them into Insurance as opinions and/or a petition.

[illegible] ... of a [illegible] annot[illegible] O. [illegible][illegible] Mr. [illegible]
Opinion, [illegible] 97751 [illegible] 584 [illegible] online 71 [illegible] 2401 [illegible]

[illegible] ... Mike White we is to fully, up by [illegible] ust[illegible] - personal
all . corrupt [illegible], ent[illegible] and [illegible]. he is but some-
[illegible] is O[illegible] a [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] is [illegible]. [illegible]
[illegible] [illegible] in [illegible] . [illegible] [illegible]
[illegible] [illegible] . [illegible] [illegible] . [illegible] do [illegible] some [illegible] is [illegible] and
[illegible] . [illegible] to [illegible] [illegible] from [illegible], [illegible], etc. general
[illegible] . to [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] is in [illegible]
with to solve a problem...See ads. Confining [illegible] of business
interest to the [illegible]...What [illegible] [illegible] is good - no load - low
load - high load, all in a context of [illegible] sight...etc.

In order to ask the Commission at [illegible] the proper Councilor as to
how I should report, I would like to ask in following, Councilor:

[illegible], if not load, if u work on ... [illegible]'s work is lobbing
and lobbing activity - is it, either . I believe half or more of my time
[illegible] of my time is not spent in lobbing activity - in my opinion, i.e.
Testing in connected in different and other [illegible]...Serving on the
State [illegible] Business and every [illegible] prior [illegible] Governor's office
(I have always fill my own travel and other expense even when this body
was under the State Department of Commerce.) Investigating the best be-
tails ... public records for the [illegible] investigation and suggestion
for ... a public that do not involve legislation. Working with new sales
reps ... tatives to help them get new accounts and renew existing members.
Reviewing various records for the small business members and publishing
this through. (I do not think it is about time that [illegible] our members
would have to register as a lobbyist?) If not, then it would suppose this
isn't lobbing; there are many other things that I could name - a great
deal of investigation time I am spending my time with in the area of insurance
retirement programs for the members, hospitalization programs for the
members, and disability income for the members - At this time we offer none
of this to the members as I have been asked the time to help study.
This was done to help the members with good information when they asked and
the feasibility of offering an Association benefits program.

I would guess that I personally spend more than half of my time lobbing
or with lobby activity.

* Only one of the other members of the SBAC (SMALL BUSINESS ADVISORY COUNCIL)
is a registered lobbyist
**Do you consider the sale of memberships lobby activity?
***Is my Newsletter lobbying?

Please let me know just how you want us to report so I can make certain that
I am doing this in a proper manner. Please answer the critical questions
to that I can understand the correct basis for your decision.

Thank you very much.

[signature]
James M. [illegible]
James Norton Gadd, President (Owner)
California Association of Independent Business

California Association of Independent Businessmen
                                                          TELEPHONE (714) 984-8424

STATE HEADQUARTERS
703 S. FERN AVENUE
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91761                                MAILING
                                                         P. O. BOX 1204
                                                         ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91762


March 2 1976


CALIFORNIA


Fair Political Practices Commission
1160 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for opinion Gov Code Sec 83114 and others

Commissioners:

Background: The California Association of Independent Business was
started in 1967. There was a need for California small businessmen to find out information about the bills being introduced in Sacramento
and a way to get their opinions on important pieces of legislation.
into legislation in Sacramento. I started the Association myself and
began to get subscribers or members from the small business community.
They could join the Association then, as now, for any amount of financial support that was acceptable to them. We knew that many small
business people were prosperous but for the most part they earned modest incomes, and this was the reason for the voluntary support. We
have taken members into the Association for nothing as they showed
great interest in this effort but simply couldn't afford any membership
dues. Our membership dues average around $30 to $35 per year.

Back in the early years of the Association I was advised by my attorney that I should
register as a lobbyist. I did this, and since I own the California
Association of Independent Business, I authorized myself as the Association lobbyist.

We now have between 4 and 5 thousand paid members in this Association. We are still set up as an independent business, just like the
members that we represent. I need to know if I am operating properly.
I think that you should know that since the beginning, I have made
every effort to comply with the law and its changes. I have had the
benefit of private Counsel with regard to proposition #9, and have
made many phone calls to the Secretary of State's office - FPP Franchise Tax-whenever that there was a problem in understanding the meaning
and spirit of my responsibilities under the law.

I have just undergone an extensive and a intensive audit by the
Franchise Tax Board under their proposition nine mandate. I think that
they will confirm that I did every thing that I could to cooperate with
them so that their task and my responsibilities could be met. This audit
took about four weeks.

My only desire is to continue this Association and represent small
business people and operate properly under the law.

Questions:

1.     Am I required to go each and every member to authorize me as their
       Lobbyist?

                                                          continued

California Association of Independent Business
TELEPHONE (714) 984-6424

STATE HEADQUARTERS
203 S. FERN AVENUE
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91761

MAILING
P.O. BOX 1304
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91762

Letter to FTC continued..

CALIFORNIA

2. As members drop out of the Association, am I required to get a letter
that dismisses me as their lobbyist?

3. Am I operating properly now?

I would like to comment: I am certainly willing to change the Association
in any way necessary in order to operate in a proper manner, if that is not
the case now. This is important because of the years of work and the hope
and trust thousand of small business owners have in this Association.
However, if I would be required to get letters from each member authorizing me as his/her lobbyist and other letters dismissing me at the time
a membership is discontinued - and be required to publish my membership list,
then this interpretation of the law would simply and effectively put me and
the Association out of business. I can see in no way that it would help the
effort for disclosure.

Please let me have your opinion as soon as possible and let me know
how and what I have to do.

Thank you very much,

James Morton Cass
James Morton Cass, President
California Association of Independent Business
201-203 South Fern Ave
Ontario, California 91761

714 984-6424

1. Am I required to ge each and every member to authorize me as their Lobbyist?

Letter to FPPC continued..

2. As members drop out of the Association, am I required to get a letter that dismisses me as their Lobbyist?

3. Am I operating properly now?

I would like to comment: I am certainly willing to change the Association in any way necessary in order to operate in a proper manner, if that is not the case now. This is important because of the years of work and the hope and trust thousands of small business members have in this Association.

However, if I would be required to get letters from each member authorizing me as his/her Lobbyist and other letters dismissing me at the time a membership is discontinued—and be required to publish my membership list, then this interpretation of the law would simply and effectively put me and the Association out of business. I can see in no way that it would help the effort for disclosure.

Please let me have your opinion as soon as possible and let me know how and what I have to do.

Thank you very much,

James Morton Gass

sections.facts:
The California Association of Independent Business was started in 1967. There was a need for California small business people to find out information about the bills being introduced in Sacramento and a way to get their opinions on important pieces of legislation, into Legislators in Sacramento. I started the Association myself, and began to get subscribers or members from the small business community. They could join the Association then, as now, for any amount of financial support that was comfortable to them. We knew that many small business people were prosperous but for the most part they earned modest incomes, and this was the reason for the voluntary support. We have taken members into the Association for nothing if they showed great interest in this effort but simply couldn't afford any membership dues. Our membership dues average around $30 to $35 per year.

Back in the early years, I was advised by my attorney that I should register as a Lobbyist. I did this, and since I owned the California Association of Independent Business, I authorized myself as the Association Lobbyist.

We now have between 4 and 5 thousand paid members in this Association. We are still set up as an independent business, just like the members that we represent. I need to know if I am operating properly.

I think that you should know that since the beginning, I have made every effort to comply with the law and its changes. I have had the benefit of private Counsel with regard to proposition #9, and have made many phone calls to the Secretary of State's office - FPPC - Franchise tax—whenever that there was a problem in understanding the meaning and spirit of my responsibilities under the law.

I have just undergone an extensive and intensive audit by the Franchise Tax Board under their proposition nine mandate. I think that they will confirm that I did every thing that I could to cooperate with them so that their task and responsibilities could be met. This audit took about four weeks.

My only desire is to continue this Association and represent small business people and operate properly under the law.

Letter to FPPC continued..

2. As members drop out of the Association, am I required to get a letter that dismisses me as their Lobbyist?

3. Am I operating properly now?

I would like to comment: I am certainly willing to change the Association in any way necessary in order to operate in a proper manner, if that is not the case now. This is important because of the years of work and the hope and trust thousands of small business members have in this Association.

However, if I would be required to get letters from each member authorizing me as his/her Lobbyist and other letters dismissing me at the time a membership is discontinued—and be required to publish my membership list, then this interpretation of the law would simply and effectively put me and the Association out of business. I can see in no way that it would help the effort for disclosure.

Please let me have your opinion as soon as possible and let me know how and what I have to do.

Thank you very much,

James Morton Gass
